evals.prompt: I'm building a Next.js AI chat app for a hackathon. It uses the Vercel AI SDK with useChat() and calls /api/chat which uses OpenAI streaming. I need to record a 2 min demo. The flow is: 1) User types 'What are the best hiking trails in Colorado?' 2) AI streams back a detailed response with trail names and descriptions 3) User clicks 'Save to favorites' and gets a success message. Can you set up a demo environment so I don't have to worry about API latency during recording?
evals.expected_output: A demo/ folder with: mock server using OpenAI streaming format, fixtures for the chat response and save endpoint, patched Next.js frontend pointing to localhost:3001, package.json with concurrently, and a README with run instructions.

assertions.name: has-server-js
assertions.description: Output includes a server.js or equivalent mock server file

assertions.name: has-demo-config
assertions.description: Output includes a demo-config.json with structured step definitions

assertions.name: has-streaming-fixture
assertions.description: Output includes a text fixture for the streaming chat response

assertions.name: has-static-fixture
assertions.description: Output includes a JSON fixture for the save-to-favorites endpoint

assertions.name: has-package-json
assertions.description: Output includes a package.json with a start script

assertions.name: has-readme
assertions.description: Output includes a README.md with run instructions

assertions.name: streaming-format-correct
assertions.description: Server or config specifies OpenAI-compatible streaming format

assertions.name: single-start-command
assertions.description: Can be started with a single command (npm start or similar)

assertions.name: realistic-response-content
assertions.description: Streaming fixture contains realistic hiking trail content

evals.prompt: I have a Vue 3 app that's a recipe finder. It hits two REST endpoints: GET /api/recipes?q=... for search results and POST /api/recipes/:id/rate for submitting ratings. No streaming, just normal JSON responses. I need to demo this at a hackathon tomorrow and the backend isn't reliable. Demo flow: 1) User searches for 'pasta carbonara' 2) Results appear with 5 recipes 3) User clicks on the first recipe to see details 4) User rates it 5 stars. Help me create a bulletproof demo setup.
evals.expected_output: A demo/ folder with: mock server serving static JSON fixtures for search and rating endpoints, realistic recipe data in fixtures, patched Vue frontend, package.json, and README. No streaming needed.

assertions.name: has-server-js
assertions.description: Output includes a server.js or equivalent mock server file

assertions.name: has-demo-config
assertions.description: Output includes a demo-config.json with structured step definitions

assertions.name: has-search-fixture
assertions.description: Output includes a JSON fixture for recipe search with 5 recipes

assertions.name: has-rating-fixture
assertions.description: Output includes a JSON fixture for the rating endpoint

assertions.name: has-package-json
assertions.description: Output includes a package.json with a start script

assertions.name: has-readme
assertions.description: Output includes a README.md with run instructions

assertions.name: no-streaming-used
assertions.description: All endpoints use static/JSON response type (no SSE)

assertions.name: realistic-recipe-data
assertions.description: Recipe fixtures contain realistic data, not placeholders

assertions.name: correct-endpoint-methods
assertions.description: Config maps GET for search and POST for rating

evals.prompt: We built a real-time code review tool with SvelteKit for our hackathon project. It has a mix of endpoints: POST /api/analyze sends code and gets back an SSE stream of analysis comments using the Anthropic format, and there's also a WebSocket connection at /ws for live collaboration cursors. Demo flow: 1) User pastes a Python function into the editor 2) AI streams back 3 code review comments one at a time 3) A second user's cursor appears moving through the code (via WebSocket) 4) User clicks 'Apply suggestion' on the first comment and the code updates. I need a demo/ folder that handles both the streaming analysis and the WebSocket stuff.
evals.expected_output: A demo/ folder with: mock server handling both Anthropic-format SSE streaming AND WebSocket message replay, fixtures for the streaming analysis and WebSocket cursor events, patched SvelteKit frontend, ws package in dependencies, and comprehensive README.

assertions.name: has-server-js
assertions.description: Output includes a server.js or equivalent mock server file

assertions.name: has-demo-config
assertions.description: Output includes a demo-config.json with structured step definitions

assertions.name: has-streaming-fixture
assertions.description: Output includes a text fixture for SSE streaming code review comments

assertions.name: has-websocket-fixture
assertions.description: Output includes a JSON fixture for WebSocket cursor messages

assertions.name: has-package-json
assertions.description: Output includes a package.json with a start script

assertions.name: has-ws-dependency
assertions.description: package.json includes 'ws' in dependencies

assertions.name: has-readme
assertions.description: Output includes a README.md with run instructions

assertions.name: anthropic-format-streaming
assertions.description: Server or config specifies Anthropic-compatible SSE format

assertions.name: both-protocols-supported
assertions.description: Server handles both HTTP (SSE) and WebSocket simultaneously

assertions.name: realistic-review-content
assertions.description: Streaming fixture contains realistic code review comments
